title: Caruelina tenuiflora
taxonomy: Caruelina tenuiflora is classified within the family Salicaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as willows and related species. It is a genus of flowering plants endemic to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Caruelina tenuiflora is further specified as Caruelina tenuiflora (Torr.) (W.A. uses: Caruelina tenuiflora is a lesser-known plant species with potential medicinal uses. Research on this plant has shown that it may possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. intro: Caruelina tenuiflora, also known as the "tall ironwood," is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Caruelina tenuiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Caruelina tenuiflora is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a topical treatment for skin conditions.
